properties.remark: MEDIA REPORTED SHINGLES ON STREET, A 12 BY 12 SHED BLOWN OVER AND A COUPLE BROKEN WINDOWS. LOCATION AT INTERECTION OF CLEMENTINE AND MICHIGAN AVENUE.
GIANT PALM TREE BRANCHES IN THE NUMBER 4 LANE OF SOUTHBOUND INTERSTATE 5 AT HARBOR DRIVE.
DOWNED TREES BLOCKING ROADWAY ON VIA DE SANTA FE.
ROCKS IN THE ROADWAY ON DEL DIOS HIGHWAY AT RANCHO DRIVE.
ROCKS IN THE ROADWAY ON STATE ROUTE 18 NEAR OLD WATERMAN CANYON ROAD.
SEVERAL LOCATIONS OF ROCKS...SMALL VEGETATION AND SOIL IN ROADWAY OF NORTH LAKE WOLFORD ROAD BETWEEN LAKE WOLFORD CAFE AND VALLEY CENETER/EAST VALLEY PARKWAY.
VERY WINDY CONDITIONS ALONG INTERSTATE 10 CORRIDOR IN THE COACHELLA VALLEY THIS EVENING. DUST BRINGING VISIBILITY DOWN TO UNDER 1/8 MILE. PHOTO INCLUDED.

KSEQ IS REPORTING A SAND STORM IMPACTED THE COACHELLA MUSIC FESTIVAL ON SUNDAY EVENING...WITH WINDS GUSTING TO NEAR 30 MPH. INDIO MESONET WAS REPORTING GUSTS TO 32 MPH AT 10 PM. THE COACHELLA MESONET WAS REPORTING GUSTS UP TO 40 MPH AT 10 PM.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL IS REPORTING LOTS OF SAND IN THE ROAD NEAR VAMER ROAD AND MONTEREY AVE.

A SPOTTER COMMUTING FROM 29 PALMS TO PALM SPRINGS THIS MORNING REPORTED THAT INDIAN AVE AT INTERSTATE 10 AND GENE AUTREY AVE AT INTERSTATE 10 WERE CLOSED...CUTTING OFF TWO MAJOR ROUTES INTO PALM SPRINGS AND CATHEDRAL CITY. VISIBILITY WAS LESS THAN A QUARTER MILE ON INTERSTATE 10 AT 7 AM.

AFTER 9 PM SUNDAY...POLICE CLOSED GENE AUTRY TRAIL BETWEEN INTERSTATE 10 AND VIA ESCUELA...AD INDIAN CANYON FROM PALM SPRINGS TRAIN ROAD TO TRAMWAY ROAD. THESE ROADS REMAINED CLOSED MONDAY MORNING DUE TO BLOWING SAND. THE BLUSTERY WINDS WHIPPED UP SAND AND KNOCKED DOWN TREES.
INCIDENT METEOROLOGISTS AT THE MOUNTAIN FIRE RELAYED A REPORT FROM FIRE DISPATCH THAT THERE WERE DEBRIS REPORTED NEAR TRAILS END.
STREAM GAUGE AT PALM CANYON CREEK REPORTED A WATER RISE OF 1.5 FEET IN A NORMALLY DRY CREEK BED AROUND 415 AM PDT. MEASURED PEAK FLOW 211 CFS AT 330 PM PDT.
INCIDENT METEOROGLOGISTS AT THE MOUNTAIN FIRE RELAYED A REPORT FROM FIRE DISPATCH WHO SAID THERE WAS 1 FOOT OF WATER CROSSING MORRIS RANCH ROAD.
INCIDENT METEOROLOGISTS AT THE MOUNTAIN FIRE RELAYED A REPORT FROM FIRE DISPATCH OF A ROCKSLIDE AND POSSIBLE DEBRIS FLOW ALONG HIGHWAY 74.
USGS GAUGE AT PALM CANYON WASH RECORDED WATER RISE OF 1 FOOT WITH A PEAK FLOW OF 399 CFS AT 545 AM PDT.
A DEBRIS FLOW FROM THE MOUNTAIN FIRE MOVED THROUGH A POND...DISPLACING THE WATER AND KILLING THE FISH.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED THAT THEY ARE CLOSING MONTEZUMA VALLEY ROAD AT MILE MARKER 14 DUE TO IT BEING UNPASSABLE BECAUSE OF DEBRIS.
CLEVELAND NF BATTALION CHIEF WAS FLAGGED DOWN BY THE PUBLIC TO REPORT DIBRIS IN THE ROADWAY ON SUNRISE, COMING FROM 79.
AT 215 PM PDT...6 INCHES OF WATER WAS FLOWING ACROSS INTERSTATE 8 AT THE EXIT OF PINE VALLEY ROAD. REPORT RELAYED FROM SPOTTER.
CREWS ON THE MOUNTAIN FIRE ARE REPORTING 18 INCHES OF WATER AND MUD ACROSS APPLE CANYON ROAD.
CLEVELAND NF BATTALION CHIEF CALLED TO REPORT 6 INCHES OF MUD AND ROCKS FLOWING ACROSS THE SUNRISE HWY...JUST NORTH OF SHRINE CAMP.

AT 1229 PM...LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED 2 AND A HALF FEET OF MUD WATER WASHING ACROSS LARSEN LANE NEAR THE CROSS-SECTION WITH DILLON ROAD IN SKY VALLEY...AND AT 250 PM...80 FEET OF DIRT AND ROCKS WERE IN THE ROADWAY...WITH THE DIRT THREE FEET THICK AND WITH MOST ROCKS BIGGER THAN BOWLING BALLS. THE ROAD WAS BEING CLOSED DUE TO IT BEING IMPASSABLE.
HIGHWAY 78 FLOODED IN TWO LOCATIONS AT YAQUI PASS WITH 30 VEHICLES TRAPPED BETWEEN THE TWO FLOODED LOCATIONS. MANY LARGE ROCKS WERE IN THE ROADWAY.
AHPS GAUGE AT PALM CANYON CREEK REPORTED A WATER RISE OF JUST OVER 2.3 FEET IN A NORMALLY DRY CREEK BED.
THE CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL IS REPORTING LARGE ROCKS IN THE ROADWAY ALONG STATE ROUTE 18 BETWEEN BIG BEAR AND LUCERNE VALLEY.
ROADWAY FLOODING AT HIGHWAY 78 AND BORREGO VALLEY ROAD.

ALONG HIGHWAY 78...THERE ARE SEVERAL LOW LYING AREAS...WHERE WATER...MUD...AND ROCKS FLOWING OVER THE ROAD. THE DEEPEST OF THE FLOWS OCCURRED ABOUT 10 MILES EAST OF SAN FELIPE ROAD...AND WAS 12 INCHES DEEP AND ABOUT 30 FEET ACROSS. NO ONE COULD PASS. ONE CAR WAS STUCK BETWEEN THIS LOW LYING AREA AND ANOTHER FLOW THAT WAS ABOUT 6 INCHES DEEP. OTHER AREAS HAD 1 TO 2 INCHES OF WATER AND DEBRIS FLOWING ACROSS THE ROAD.

SPOTTER CALLED TO REPORT STREET FLOODING NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF OLD WOMAN SPRINGS RD/HIGHWAY 247 AND LOCUST ST. LOCUST ST WAS IMPASSABLE WITH 1 FOOT OF WATER ESTIMATED TO BE FLOWING OVER THE HIGHWAY.

SPOTTER CALLED TO REPORT STREET FLOODING NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF OLD WOMAN SPRINGS RD/HIGHWAY 247 AND LOCUST ST. LOCUST ST WAS IMPASSABLE WITH 1 FOOT OF WATER ESTIMATED TO BE FLOWING OVER THE HIGHWAY.
LANDSPOUT LOOKING TORNADO PICTURE SUBMITED TO OUR TWITTER ACCOUNT. THE PHOTO WAS TAKEN ON THE WEST SIDE OF THE STORM NEAR HELENDALE.
USGS STREAM GAGE AT PALM CANYON CREEK REPORTED A 2.7 FT. RISE BETWEEN 4 AND 5 PM PDT WITH AN ESTIMATED 363 CFS.
ARABY ROAD CLOSED AT THE WASH DUE TO MOVING WATER AND MUD COVERING THE ROADWAY AROUND 645 PM.
MOVIE THEATER IN RANCHO MIRAGE FLOODED AND EVACUATED.
HIGHWAY 111 AT INDIAN TRAIL DRIVE HAS 1 FOOT OF WATER IN THE ROADWAY AT THE GUTTERS.
INTERSECTION OF PAISANO ROAD AND CHOLLA WAY WASHED OUT WITH FLASH FLOODING OCCURRING.
LOW WATER CROSSINGS FLOODED OVER HIGHWAY S-22 BETWEEN BORREGO SPRINGS AND RANCHITA. NUMEROUS LARGE BOULDERS BLOCKED THE ROADWAY WITH OTHER DEBRIS AND RUNNING WATER IN THE ROADWAY.
18 INCHES OF WATER OVER HIGHWAY S-2 NEAR CANEBRAKE.
18 INCHES OF WATER OVER HIGHWAY S-2 AT VALLECITOS CREEK.
WIDESPREAD FLOODING REPORTED IN INDIO. SEVERAL ROADS CLOSED WITH NUMEROUS MOTORISTS STRANDED.
FAST FLOWING WATER ON YUKIE ROAD IN BORREGO SPRINGS. LOW WATER CROSSINGS FLOODED.
WATER...DEBRIS AND BOULDERS FLOWING ACROSS HIGHWAY 62 JUST NORTH OF N INDIAN CANYON DRIVE. AREA FLOWING ACROSS HIGHWAY IS 40 FEET WIDE...AND 6 FEET DEEP.
DEBRIS FROM FLOODING WAS OBSERVED ACROSS PORTIONS OF STATE ROUTE 79 BETWEEN INTERSTATE 8 AND LAKE CUYAMACA.
FLASH FLOODING WAS REPORTED ON BORREGO SPRINGS ROAD...JUST SOUTH OF SAN PABLO ROAD VIA FACEBOOK. FLOODING ACROSS THE ROAD CONTINUED THROUGH 9 PM PDT.
SIGNIFICANT MUD AND DEBRIS WAS REPORTED AT THE AGUA CALIENTE SPRINGS AIRPORT...AND THE AGUA CALIENTE COUNTY PARK.
REPORTED MAJOR FLOODING IN DRY WASHES AND DEBRIS REMOVAL REQUIRED ON HIGHWAY 18 TOWARDS BIG BEAR AT INTERSECTION OF CAMPO ROAD. WATER AS DEEP AS 2 FEET IN PLACES. FLASH FLOOD DURATION 3 HOURS
WATER OVER HIGHWAY 18, ESTIMATED BY THE SPOTTER TO BE 50FT WIDE AND +2FT DEEP. FLOW WAS CARRYING BOULDERS ACROSS THE ROAD.
FIRE RESCUE OFFICIALS REPORTED THAT PORTIONS OF VALLEY OF THE FALLS DRIVE WERE CLOSED DUE TO WATER FLOWING OVER THE ROAD FROM CLARKS CREEK AND PROSPECT CREEK.
LARGE TREE FEEL ON THE RUBIDOUX BLVD OFFRAMP ALONG HIGHWAY 60.
60 FREEWAY FLOODED...WITH 4 TO 5 VEHICLES STUCK IN FLOOD WATERS UNDER THE MARKET OVERPASS. ONE MOTORIST IS REPORTING THAT THE FLOOD WATERS ARE MOVING HIS VEHICLE.
ESTIMATED WIND GUST UP TO 70 MPH AT UCR. VERY HEAVY RAIN WITH REDUCED VISIBILITIES.
FIRE RESCUE OFFICIALS REPORTED THAT PROSPECT AND CLARKS CREEK WERE OUT OF THE BANKS.
HIGHWAY 91, LANES 2, 3, AND 4 FLOODED, WITH VEHICLES FLOODED OUT AND SLIDING.
2 1/2 MILES OF ROUTE 247 REQUIRED MUD AND DEBRIS REMOVAL BY HEAVY EQUIPMENT. RESIDUAL MUD AS DEEP AS 4 INCHES. WATER REPORTED FLOWING OVER THE ROADWAY. TIME ESTIMATED. FLASH FLOOD DURATION 2 HOURS
KTLA SHOWED HELICOPTER FOOTAGE OF RAGGING DRY WASH AND FLOODING IN PHELAN. FLASH FLOOD DURATION 2 HOURS
CHP REPORTS FLOODED ROADWAY AND DEBRIS INCLUDING ROCKS ON ROUTE 243. NORTH BOUND TRAFFIC CLOSED.
SILENT VALLEY RV PARK HAD STRANDED RESIDENTS FROM DEBRIS FLOW AND FLOODING. FLASH FLOOD DURATION 2 HOURS
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED ORANGE AVENUE FROM MURRIETA ROAD TO EVANS ROAD BEING COMPLETELY FLOODED AND VEHICLES NOT ABLE TO MAKE IT THROUGH.
SPOTTER REPORT OF 6 INCHES OF WATER FLOWING OVER HIGHWAY 247 AT RECHE ROAD. WIND GUST TO 50 MPH.
METAL SHED BLOWN APART AND POWERLINES DOWN. POWER OUTAGE. THUNDER OBSERVED.
USGS STREAM GAGE ON PALM CANYON CREEK REPORTED A 3.19 FT. RISE. GAGE PEAKED AT 3 PM PDT.
ARABY DRIVE IN PALM SPRINGS SHUT DOWN DUE TO A WALL OF WATER THAT PASSED THROUGH THE AREA.
CHP REPORTED WATER FLOWING ACROSS THE ROADWAY ALONG STATE ROUTE 74 E / STATE ROUTE 371 IN MOUNTAIN CENTER. THE ROAD IS IMPASSABLE.

SPOTTER SDG 229 REPORTED A DEBRIS FLOW LESS THAN AN HOUR OLD AT BUTTERFIELD RANCH ALONG S2. DEBRIS FLOW CONSISTED OF MUD AND 1 TO 2 INCH ROCKS ACROSS THE ROAD. ROAD WAS PASSABLE WHEN SPOTTER CAME UPON IT, HALF HOUR TO AN HOUR LATER.
FLASH FLOOD AND DEBRIS FLOW DOWN TAHQUITZ CREEK THROUGH THE TAHQUITZ CREEK GOLF RESORT.
CATHEDRAL CANYON DR CLOSED AT WHITEWATER WASH DUE TO FLOODING AND DEBRIS FLOW.
AT 1132 AM PDT SKY VALLEY MESONET REPORTED WINDS SUSTAINED 41 MPH GUSTING TO 61 MPH FROM THE SSE. WINDS CONTINUED TO GUST OVER 50 MPH THROUGH 1212 PM PDT.
APPLE CANYON ROAD AND BONITA VISTA CANYON ROADS FLOODED. THESE ARE ON THE SOUTH AND WEST END OF THE MOUNTAIN WILDFIRE PERIMETER.
SDGE BARRETT RAWS MEASURED GUST TO 70 MPH. COLLAPSING THUNDERSTORM IN VICINTY PER WSR-88D RADAR. RAWS CALIBRATED 3 WEEKS AGO.
ONE SMALL TREE DOWN, BRANCHES AND DEBRIS ON BARRETT LAKE ROAD.
SDGE BARRETT RAWS MEASURED GUST TO 70 MPH. COLLAPSING THUNDERSTORM IN VICINTY PER WSR-88D RADAR
FLOOD FLOOD AND DEBRIS FLOW 2 FEET DEEP AND 20 FEET WIDE FLOWING ACROSS GOLF CLUB DRIVE.
SDGE REPORTS POWER POLES DOWN AND A FEW TREES DOWN. POWER OUTAGE.
COUNTY REPORTED TREE DOWN BLOCKING DEER CREEK TRAIL
COUNTY REPORTED TREE DOWN BLOCKING ROADWAY
LARGE BOULDER IN THE ROAD ON SUNRISE HIGHWAY ABOUT 2 MILES NORTH OF INTERSTATE 8...WITH SEVERAL SMALLER ONES IN THE ROADWAY AS WELL.

CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED A SMALL ROCKSLIDE AT WESLEY ROAD AND HIGHWAY 243.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED A DEBRIS FLOW ON HIGHWAY 74 AT MCCALL ROAD. THE WATER CLEARED OUT SOON AFTER AND LEFT BEHIND DEBRIS ON THE ROAD...SUCH AS ROCKS AND STICKS.

CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED 5 OR 6 SPOTS OF WATER RUNNING ACROSS HIGHWAY 94 BETWEEN SHOCKEY TRUCK TRAIL AND SHERIDAN ROADS...WITH A MAXIMUM WATER DEPTH OF 6 INCHES.

CHP REPORTED GOLF CLUB DRIVE CLOSED AT THE WASH DUE TO FLOODING.
